commands.name: /jira
commands.description: Fetch a Jira ticket and orchestrate full implementation from assignment to resolution

arguments.name: ticket_key_or_mode
arguments.description: Jira ticket key (e.g. FO-2847), or a mode keyword: sprint, teams, watch, resume

flags.description: Inject high-priority developer guidance that supplements the ticket description and is passed to all implementation agents
flags.example: /jira FO-2847 --add-context "Use the existing v2 API pattern"

modes.name: ticket
modes.description: Single ticket mode â€” fetch, analyze, plan, implement, and update the ticket

modes.name: sprint
modes.description: Batch sprint mode â€” fetch all open sprint tickets, let user pick one or more to implement

modes.name: teams
modes.description: List all available Scrum teams from Jira and display them

modes.name: watch
modes.description: Watch mode â€” monitor a ticket's status and react to changes

modes.name: resume
modes.description: Resume mode â€” continue implementation of a previously started ticket

modes.name: review_feedback
modes.description: Review feedback mode â€” address specific review comments on a ticket

commands.name: /jam
commands.description: Analyze JAM (jam.dev) recordings to understand bugs and feature requests captured in video

arguments.name: reference
arguments.description: A JAM URL, JAM ID, or Jira ticket key (which will be searched for JAM links)

modes.name: jam_url
modes.description: Directly analyze a JAM recording by URL or ID

modes.name: jira_ticket
modes.description: Look up the Jira ticket, find embedded JAM links, then analyze them

requires: JAM_API_TOKEN in .env (optional â€” falls back to WebFetch)

commands.name: /tempo
commands.description: Quick Jira time tracking â€” log or view time on tickets without opening the browser

arguments.name: action
arguments.description: Action to perform: addTime or getTime

arguments.name: ticket
arguments.description: Jira ticket key to log time against

arguments.name: time_range_or_duration
arguments.description: Clock range (14:00 16:00) or shorthand duration (2h, 45m, 1h30m) â€” required for addTime

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: Quoted description of the work done â€” required for addTime

modes.name: addTime
modes.description: Log time to a ticket with clock range or duration shorthand

modes.name: getTime
modes.description: View all logged time on a ticket in a formatted table

commands.name: /create
commands.description: Context-aware feature creator that adapts role to the project type (game, SaaS, API, mobile, application)

arguments.name: feature_description
arguments.description: Natural language description of the feature to implement
examples: Monsters that hunt the player using 3D models
Add a dark mode toggle to the settings page
REST endpoint for bulk invoice export

modes.name: role_adaptation
modes.trigger: Automatic â€” reads ProjectType from .claude/.env or ~/.claude/.env
modes.description: Assumes the correct senior developer role based on ProjectType (GAME, APPLICATION, SAAS, API, MOBILE) and detected tech stack

requires: ProjectType in .claude/.env (optional â€” auto-detects stack if missing)

commands.name: /bug
commands.description: Context-aware bug fixer with screenshot analysis â€” investigates, diagnoses, fixes, and verifies

arguments.name: bug_description
arguments.description: Natural language description of the bug; screenshots pasted in conversation are also analyzed
examples: Player falls through the floor when jumping near walls
Login page shows 500 error after session timeout
Invoice PDF export generates blank pages for multi-line items

modes.name: screenshot_analysis
modes.trigger: Automatic when screenshots are pasted into the conversation alongside the command
modes.description: Reads and analyzes pasted screenshots for error messages, stack traces, UI state, and console output

commands.name: /changelog
commands.description: Compile all unprocessed AI agent reports into a styled HTML changelog that matches the project design

modes.name: project_themed
modes.trigger: Automatic â€” reads .claude/project-profile.json for design settings
modes.description: Generates a changelog styled to match the project's colors, fonts, and theme (falls back to auto-detecting CSS variables or package.json)

requires: .claude/unprocessed_reports/ â€” must contain at least one .md report

commands.name: /new-feature
commands.description: Six-phase feature development pipeline â€” plan, UI design in Paper, parallel frontend/backend implementation, report

arguments.name: feature_request
arguments.description: Natural language description of the feature to build â€” the pipeline handles planning, design, implementation, and reporting
examples: Add product search with filters and sorting
User notification system with email and in-app alerts

requires: Project paths (will ask if not found)
Paper MCP (optional â€” for UI mockups)

commands.name: /code-analysis
commands.description: Analyze only the current session's code changes (git diff) for security issues, logic errors, and code quality

arguments.name: context
arguments.description: Optional context about the changes being reviewed

requires: Git repository with uncommitted or recent changes

commands.name: /dev-team
commands.description: Full iterative dev team loop â€” security audit + quality scan + fix + test, repeated until zero findings

commands.name: /security-audit
commands.description: OWASP Top 10 security scan â€” injection, broken auth, XSS, data exposure, access control, misconfiguration

commands.name: /quality-audit
commands.description: Code quality scan â€” dead code, type safety, performance, error handling, API design, maintainability

commands.name: /fix-all
commands.description: Fix all issues found by the security and quality auditors â€” reads reports and applies fixes in CRITICAL > WARNING > INFO order

commands.name: /test-all
commands.description: Write and run comprehensive unit, security, and regression tests for all recent fixes until 100% pass

commands.name: /master-report
commands.description: Compile all agent reports into a single polished HTML master report with dashboard, findings, fixes, and test results

commands.name: /unit-test
commands.description: Multi-language unit test engineer â€” scan coverage gaps, write tests, and run them across any project stack

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Test target: * for full project scan, a file path for single-file tests, or --fix-ignored to fix skipped tests

arguments.name: project_name_or_path
arguments.description: Optional project name or path â€” resolves against known projects or common parent paths

flags.description: Find all ignored, disabled, or skipped tests and let the user select which to fix

modes.name: full_scan
modes.description: Scan the entire project, map coverage gaps, and write tests for all uncovered code

modes.name: single_file
modes.description: Write unit tests for a single file or class only

modes.name: fix_ignored
modes.description: Find all @Ignore, .skip, xdescribe, or disabled tests â€” list them and let user choose which to fix

commands.name: /playwright-test
commands.description: Run full Playwright E2E test suite, fix failures, identify coverage gaps, and write new tests
requires: Playwright installed in demo-api/ (auto-installs if missing)
App running or Docker container on port 3000

commands.name: /deps
commands.description: Dependency health audit â€” vulnerabilities (CVEs), outdated packages, license risks across any project stack

arguments.name: project_name_or_path
arguments.description: Optional project name or path â€” uses current directory if not specified

flags.description: Only scan for CVE vulnerabilities â€” skip license and outdated checks
Only check for newer package versions available
Only check license compatibility risks

modes.name: full
modes.description: Full audit covering vulnerabilities, outdated packages, and license risks

modes.name: vuln_only
modes.description: Vulnerability-only scan focused on CVEs and security advisories

modes.name: outdated
modes.description: Check only for newer versions of installed packages

modes.name: license
modes.description: Check only for license compatibility risks (GPL, AGPL, etc.)

commands.name: /verify
commands.description: E2E verification with Playwright â€” verifies the latest changes work in the live app using the project profile

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Empty = auto-verify latest report; path = verify specific report; 'setup' = configure profile; text = verify described thing
examples: Check that login redirects to dashboard

modes.name: auto
modes.description: Read the most recent unprocessed report and verify the changes described in it

modes.name: specific
modes.description: Verify the changes described in a specific report file

modes.name: setup
modes.description: Interactive project profile builder â€” sets up frontend URL, auth type, selectors, and test credentials

modes.name: manual
modes.description: Verify a specific behavior described in free text

commands.name: /git
commands.description: Git operations manager â€” sync branches, resolve merge conflicts, and report branch status across projects

arguments.name: operation
arguments.description: Operation to perform: sync or status

arguments.name: project_name_or_path
arguments.description: Optional project name or path â€” uses current directory if not specified

flags.description: Attempt automatic conflict resolution after merge
Apply the sync operation to all known projects in PROJECT_MAP

modes.name: sync
modes.description: Merge latest from the specified branch into the current branch

modes.name: status
modes.description: Show branch status â€” current branch, uncommitted changes, remote comparison â€” for current or all projects

commands.name: /repo-setup
commands.description: Clone and fully set up a repository â€” analyze stack, install dependencies, configure env, build, and verify

arguments.name: repo_uri_or_org
arguments.description: Git repo URI, GitHub org/user URL, local path, or empty for current directory

flags.description: Clone and report on the repo structure without installing anything
Clone, install, configure, build, and test all repos without prompting
When scanning an org, only process repos whose name, description, or README matches this text
Switch to a specific GitHub account before API calls and cloning
Skip cloning and instead scan the local filesystem for repos belonging to the org

modes.name: single_repo
modes.description: Full setup of a single repository â€” clone, analyze, install, configure, build, verify

modes.name: org_scan
modes.description: Scan all repositories in a GitHub organization or user account

modes.name: local_repo
modes.description: Analyze an already-cloned local repository

modes.name: current_dir
modes.description: Analyze the repository in the current working directory

commands.name: /impact-scan
commands.description: Cross-repository impact scanner â€” analyze where a change needs to be made across an entire codebase or org

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: Change request description â€” what needs to be changed across the codebase
examples: Add product price to payload response
Rename customerId to clientId across all services

flags.description: Scan all repos in a GitHub organization
Scan repos already on disk at the given path
Comma-separated list of specific repo names or paths to scan
Apply mode â€” read a previous scan report and implement ALL changes locally
Target directory for cloning repos when using --apply (default: current directory)
Parse an existing HTML impact scan report and generate a structured JSON file from it
Switch to a specific GitHub account for API calls

modes.name: scan
modes.description: Scan current org/project context to find all files that need changing

modes.name: org_scan
modes.description: Fetch all repos from a GitHub org and scan them all

modes.name: local_scan
modes.description: Scan repos already on disk at the given path

modes.name: apply
modes.description: Read a previously generated impact scan report and implement all the described changes

modes.name: convert_json
modes.description: Parse an existing HTML report and extract all data into a structured JSON file

commands.name: /report
commands.description: Analyze all code changes on the current branch and generate an HTML report with testing instructions â€” optionally upload to Jira

arguments.name: ticket_key
arguments.description: Optional Jira ticket key â€” if provided, the report is automatically uploaded to that ticket

flags.description: Analyze changes since a specific commit hash
Compare current branch against a specific branch instead of the auto-detected base

modes.name: auto
modes.description: Analyze current branch vs its auto-detected base branch (main/master/develop)

modes.name: auto_upload
modes.description: Analyze and automatically upload the report to the specified Jira ticket

modes.name: since_commit
modes.description: Analyze changes starting from a specific commit

modes.name: compare_branch
modes.description: Compare the current branch against a specific branch

requires: JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_TOKEN, JIRA_BASE_URL in .env (for Jira upload)

commands.name: /create-project
commands.description: From idea to running application â€” ask clarifying questions, design architecture + UI in Paper, get approval, then build the full project with a team of agents

arguments.name: project_idea
arguments.description: Natural language description of the project to build from scratch
examples: A task management app with Kanban boards and team collaboration
REST API for a pet adoption service with PostgreSQL
A 2D platformer game with procedural level generation
SaaS dashboard for monitoring IoT devices

modes.name: full_pipeline
modes.description: Full pipeline: clarifying Q&A -> architecture design -> UI mockups in Paper -> HTML plan -> user approval -> parallel agent implementation -> testing -> deployment

requires: Paper MCP (optional â€” for UI mockups)

commands.name: /full-pipeline
commands.description: Complete delivery pipeline â€” code quality loop, Playwright E2E, Docker build + deploy + integration tests, master report

commands.name: /docker-build
commands.description: Build, validate, and security-scan the Docker image â€” checks size, non-root user, health check, and CVEs

commands.name: /docker-deploy
commands.description: Deploy the application with Docker Compose, wait for health check, verify endpoints, and report all access info

commands.name: /docker-test
commands.description: Run comprehensive integration tests against a live Docker deployment â€” auth flow, CRUD, security, edge cases
requires: Running Docker container on port 3000 (deploy via /docker-deploy first)

commands.name: /docker-teardown
commands.description: Safely tear down the Docker deployment, clean up containers and networks, and report freed resources
requires: Running containers from docker-compose.yml in demo-api/

commands.name: /api-discover
commands.description: Discover all API endpoints on a domain by scanning org repos. Generates Swagger-like HTML documentation.

arguments.name: endpoint_url
arguments.description: API endpoint URL or domain pattern

flags.description: GitHub org URL to scan repos from
Path to scan for local repos
Single repo path to scan
Use a specific GitHub account
Filter repos by name/README content
Include private/internal endpoints
Re-open a previously generated report

modes.name: discover
modes.trigger: Default — provide a URL or pattern
modes.description: Scans repos for all endpoints matching the domain

modes.name: view
modes.description: Opens a previously generated report in the browser
